title: Microglia specific drug targeting using natural products for the regulation of redox imbalance in neurodegeneration
impact: Natural products as targeted microglial therapeutics for oxidative stress
problem: Neurodegeneration involves redox imbalance but specific microglial drug targeting using natural compounds remained unexplored.
method: Systematic review and analysis of natural product interactions with microglia-specific pathways regulating redox homeostasis.
results: Identified key natural compounds capable of modulating microglial redox signalling with therapeutic potential in neurodegeneration.
impact_long: Cited 51 times — established natural product-microglia axis as a viable therapeutic direction in neurodegeneration research.

title: Pax6 influences expression patterns of genes involved in neuro-degeneration
impact: First evidence linking Pax6 transcription factor to neurodegeneration-associated gene networks
problem: The role of Pax6 in regulating genes associated with neurodegenerative pathways in the brain was completely unknown.
method: Expression profiling of Pax6-regulated genes in brain tissue with bioinformatic analysis of neurodegenerative gene networks.
results: Pax6 was found to influence expression of multiple genes associated with neurodegeneration, establishing a novel regulatory axis.
impact_long: Cited 33 times — opened an entirely new line of inquiry linking developmental transcription factors to neurodegeneration.

title: Targeting mitochondria in the regulation of neurodegenerative diseases: A comprehensive review
impact: Positioned mitochondria as central therapeutic targets across multiple neurodegenerative conditions
problem: Mitochondrial dysfunction in neurodegeneration was studied in silos per disease — no unified mechanistic framework existed.
method: Comprehensive review synthesising mitochondrial pathology across Alzheimer's, Parkinson's and ALS with therapeutic targeting strategies.
results: Established a unified framework of mitochondrial dysfunction across neurodegenerative diseases with actionable therapeutic targets.
impact_long: Cited 30 times — now used as a reference framework in mitochondria-targeted drug discovery programs.

title: Pax6 binds to promoter sequence elements associated with immunological surveillance and energy homeostasis in brain of aging mice
impact: Revealed Pax6 as a regulator of brain immune surveillance during aging
problem: Whether Pax6 directly regulates promoters of immune and metabolic genes in the aging brain was entirely uncharacterised.
method: ChIP and promoter binding analysis of Pax6 in aging mouse brain tissue across multiple age groups.
results: Pax6 directly binds promoters of genes controlling immunological surveillance and energy homeostasis with age-dependent changes.
impact_long: Cited 23 times — bridged developmental neuroscience and neuroimmunology through Pax6 regulatory mechanisms.

title: Transcriptional and epigenetic regulation of microglia in maintenance of brain homeostasis and neurodegeneration
impact: Comprehensive map of how gene regulation controls microglial identity and disease states
problem: The transcriptional and epigenetic mechanisms governing microglial homeostasis versus disease states were poorly understood.
method: Systematic review of transcriptomic, epigenomic and single-cell studies of microglia across homeostatic and neurodegenerative conditions.
results: Identified key transcriptional regulators and epigenetic marks controlling microglial states with implications for therapeutic reprogramming.
impact_long: Cited 22 times — serves as a key reference for microglial epigenomics and transcriptional control in neurodegeneration.

title: Unveiling the role of gut-brain axis in regulating neurodegenerative diseases: A comprehensive review
impact: Established gut-brain communication as a modifiable axis in neurodegeneration
problem: The mechanistic contribution of gut-brain signalling to neurodegeneration onset and progression lacked systematic synthesis.
method: Comprehensive review of gut microbiome, vagal signalling, and neuroinflammation literature with mechanistic pathway analysis.
results: Mapped gut-brain axis mechanisms relevant to Alzheimer's and Parkinson's with identification of microbiome-based intervention targets.
impact_long: Cited 44 times — one of the most cited works from the lab, driving interest in microbiome-targeted neuroprotection.

title: Trends in neuroimmunology: cross-talk between brain-resident and peripheral immune cells in both health and disease
impact: Defined the neuroimmune dialogue between central and peripheral immune systems
problem: How brain-resident immune cells and peripheral immune populations communicate in health versus neurodegeneration was poorly mapped.
method: Multi-author international review synthesising neuroimmunology research on microglia, astrocytes and peripheral immune cell interactions.
results: Established a framework for brain-peripheral immune crosstalk with implications for neuroinflammatory disease intervention.
impact_long: Cited 6 times within months of publication — rapidly becoming a key reference in the emerging neuroimmunology field.

title: Lipopolysaccharide-induced inflammation modulates testicular kisspeptin system in mice
impact: First evidence of neuroinflammation affecting the reproductive neuroendocrine axis
problem: Whether systemic LPS-induced neuroinflammation affects the hypothalamic-gonadal kisspeptin system was unknown.
method: LPS-induced inflammation model in mice with analysis of kisspeptin expression and signalling in testicular tissue.
results: LPS inflammation significantly modulated testicular kisspeptin system, revealing a neuroinflammation-reproduction axis.
impact_long: Most recent publication — opens a new research direction connecting neuroimmunology to reproductive endocrinology.
